name: Samgyetang (Baby Chicken & Ginseng Soup)
recipeIngredient: 2 Pieces fresh ginger; each -2 inches long
1 small Chicken OR cornish game hen (about 1 pound)
recipeInstructions:
This Samgyetang is a celebrated soup from the island of Cheju, and deserves to be enjoyed since the prestigious, almost mystical, ginseng root is cooked and eaten like a vegetable with the chicken. The Samgyetang is served in a tukbaege and brought still bubbling to the table. It is considered a summer dish in restaurants and homes. Koreans will break up the chicken and rice in the pot so that it becomes a gruel. I prefer that each person helps themselves from the pot, taking something of all the ingredients and spooning the rich, thick broth over all. The jujubes provide a touch of contrasting sweetness to the chicken, rice, and ginseng. Ginseng, or "insam" as it is also called in Korea, tastes like a slightly bitter parsnip. Its properties are alleged to be strengthening and bring about rejuvenation.
1. Mix the rice, ginseng root, scallion, 3 of the jujubes, and 1 garlic clove together and stuff the chicken. Sew up the opening.
2. Place the chicken in a tukbaege or heavy pot with a cover that is just slightly larger than the chicken. Add the remaining 2 cups water, the other 3 jujubes, the other garlic clove, sesame seeds, sesame oil, and pepper. Bring to a boil, cover the pot, and reduce heat to low.
3. Simmer over low heat for 1 hour. The soup will develop a thick, cloudy consistency and the chicken will soften enough to melt away from the bones.
4. Serve hot in the tukbaege, or transfer the chicken and broth to a large serving bowl.
Serves 2 with a variety of side dishes.
